title: Drip vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Moosend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your business needs.
intro: Drip and Moosend are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different business types. Drip specializes in e-commerce integration with revenue tracking, while Moosend offers a freemium model ideal for small businesses and startups. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep integration with revenue tracking and attribution
You want SMS marketing capabilities included alongside email
You prioritize onsite campaigns and visual automation builder for complex workflows
choose_b_if: You're a small business or startup with a limited budget
You want to start with a free tier and scale affordably as you grow
You need unlimited email sends and prefer straightforward pricing without surprises

faq.a: It depends on your business type. Drip is superior for e-commerce companies needing sophisticated revenue tracking and store integration. Moosend excels for small businesses and startups seeking affordability, a free tier, and straightforward email automation. Both are well-regarded, but Moosend has slightly higher customer satisfaction ratings overall.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Moosend?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, especially for small businesses. It offers a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers with unlimited emails, then starts at just $9/month for paid plans. Drip requires a $39/month minimum with no free option, making it pricier upfront but better suited for established e-commerce operations.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, switching between platforms is possible. Both tools support standard email list exports and imports. However, you may lose some automation workflows and customizations in the transfer. It's recommended to plan your migration carefully and test workflows in your new platform before fully migrating.
